speaker: DUNCAN
text: Is execution done on Cawdor? Are not

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Those in commission yet returned?

speaker: MALCOLM
text: My liege,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: They are not yet come back. But I have spoke

speaker: MALCOLM
text: With one that saw him die, who did report

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That very frankly he confessed his treasons,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Implored your Highness’ pardon, and set forth

speaker: MALCOLM
text: A deep repentance. Nothing in his life

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Became him like the leaving it. He died

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As one that had been studied in his death

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To throw away the dearest thing he owed

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As ’twere a careless trifle.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: There’s no art

speaker: DUNCAN
text: To find the mind’s construction in the face.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: He was a gentleman on whom I built

speaker: DUNCAN
text: An absolute trust.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: O worthiest cousin,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: The sin of my ingratitude even now

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Was heavy on me. Thou art so far before

speaker: DUNCAN
text: That swiftest wing of recompense is slow

speaker: DUNCAN
text: To overtake thee. Would thou hadst less deserved,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: That the proportion both of thanks and payment

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Might have been mine! Only I have left to say,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: More is thy due than more than all can pay.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The service and the loyalty I owe

speaker: MACBETH
text: In doing it pays itself. Your Highness’ part

speaker: MACBETH
text: Is to receive our duties, and our duties

speaker: MACBETH
text: Are to your throne and state children and servants,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which do but what they should by doing everything

speaker: MACBETH
text: Safe toward your love and honor.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Welcome hither.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: I have begun to plant thee and will labor

speaker: DUNCAN
text: To make thee full of growing.—Noble Banquo,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: That hast no less deserved nor must be known

speaker: DUNCAN
text: No less to have done so, let me enfold thee

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And hold thee to my heart.

speaker: BANQUO
text: There, if I grow,

speaker: BANQUO
text: The harvest is your own.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: My plenteous joys,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Wanton in fullness, seek to hide themselves

speaker: DUNCAN
text: In drops of sorrow.—Sons, kinsmen, thanes,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And you whose places are the nearest, know

speaker: DUNCAN
text: We will establish our estate upon

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Our eldest, Malcolm, whom we name hereafter

speaker: DUNCAN
text: The Prince of Cumberland; which honor must

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Not unaccompanied invest him only,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: But signs of nobleness, like stars, shall shine

speaker: DUNCAN
text: On all deservers.—From hence to Inverness

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And bind us further to you.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The rest is labor which is not used for you.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I’ll be myself the harbinger and make joyful

speaker: MACBETH
text: The hearing of my wife with your approach.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So humbly take my leave.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: My worthy Cawdor.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The Prince of Cumberland! That is a step

speaker: MACBETH
text: On which I must fall down or else o’erleap,

speaker: MACBETH
text: For in my way it lies. Stars, hide your fires;

speaker: MACBETH
text: Let not light see my black and deep desires.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The eye wink at the hand, yet let that be

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which the eye fears, when it is done, to see.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: True, worthy Banquo. He is full so valiant,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And in his commendations I am fed:

speaker: DUNCAN
text: It is a banquet to me.—Let’s after him,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Whose care is gone before to bid us welcome.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: It is a peerless kinsman.
